name: QUALCOMM Incorporated
summary: QUALCOMM Incorporated (QCOM, Information Technology) — composite stance BULLISH. Latest reported quarter FY2026 Q1: revenue $10.6B (-3.5% YoY), net margin 69.5%. Management credibility 1.00 on 6 claims. Valuation 15.0x forward P/E (-43% vs peers). Spot $201.23 (above 200-DMA).

bull: Profitability premium — net margin of 69.5% in the latest quarter sits well above the cross-sector ~10–15% benchmark, indicative of either structural pricing power or operating leverage from scale. Expect this to compress only modestly through-cycle.
Margin trajectory — net margin expanded +46.9pp over the last 4 quarters in the dataset. Management's cost-out commentary on recent calls is showing up in the P&L, not just the press release.

Management credibility — MCS of 1.00 on 6 tracked forward claims (2B/0H/0M record). When management says they will deliver, they typically do — a meaningful re-rating support that is often overlooked in quantitative screens.

Trend regime constructive — spot $201.23 sits +26.4% above the 200-DMA ($159.24). The long-term moving average has been a reliable reference for institutional capital and remains supportive of the directional bias.

Range-positioning balanced — at 68% of the 52-week range ($124.07–$237.53), the setup is neither at a momentum-exhaustion ceiling nor at a capitulation floor. Risk/reward into the next catalyst window screens reasonable.

Valuation discount to peers — 15.0x forward P/E versus peer median 26.5x — a 43% discount. Unless there is a fundamental quality gap that justifies the discount, the gap should compress over time as the cycle plays out.
bear: Top-line in contraction — revenue down -3.5% YoY in FY2026 Q1. The question is whether this is the trough of a cyclical drawdown or the start of secular decline. Without a stabilization read in the next 1–2 quarters, the multiple is at risk of de-rating before earnings catches a bid.
trigger_up: Revenue growth re-accelerates above 8% YoY on next print — confirms the demand environment hasn't degraded into the back half.
Net margin expands above 71.0% — confirms pricing power and/or cost discipline thesis.
Management raises FY guide on next quarterly call — direct upward revision to consensus.
trigger_down: Revenue growth decelerates below 0% YoY — signals demand has materially softened versus the latest disclosed run-rate.
Net margin compresses below 67.5% — flags either input-cost pressure or competitive dynamics shifting unfavorably.
Management cuts FY guide on next quarterly call — direct downward revision; historically sees 5–15% drawdown on print.
Decisive break below the 52-week low at $124.07 — technical confirmation that the long-term trend has rolled over.
bottom_line:
BULLISH. QUALCOMM Incorporated (QCOM) printed $10.6B in revenue in FY2026 Q1 (-3.5% YoY), net margin of 69.5%. Management credibility is strong (MCS 1.00, 2B/0H/0M record), supporting take-up of the forward guide. At 15.0x forward P/E, the stock trades at a 43% discount to peers (26.5x median) — the gap is either an opportunity or a warning, depending on whether the quality gap with peers is structural.

Technical regime is constructive: spot $201.23 above the 200-DMA ($159.24, +26.4%) with neutral momentum (RSI 67). Sell-side consensus PT of $195 implies -3.1% upside. 30-day implied move is ±19.3%; 90-day is ±33.4%. Position-sizing should reflect conviction; use the trigger list to manage adds and trims around the next earnings catalyst.
